reasoning.App.tsx: Both /files and /admin routes now render the same FilesAdminView component instance instead of FilesView/AdminView; nav links and route paths unchanged (no link rot).
reasoning.App.test.tsx: Router test's per-route assertions for /files and /admin (heading + files-status/admin-status testids sourced from mocked mockClient) no longer apply once FilesAdminView performs real fetch() calls -- mirrors the precedent App.test.tsx's own comment already documents for /query (6.1.2) and /graph (6.1.3).
reasoning.mockClient.ts: Left unmodified, confirmed by checking 71cb1bb/7bb8af4 diffs: fetchQueryResult/fetchGraphNeighbors stubs were left in place (dead but harmless) once QueryView/GraphView started calling fetch() directly, so fetchFilesCatalog/fetchAdminStats are left untouched here for the same precedent -- no import of mockClient remains in FilesAdminView.tsx.

dependents.QueryView.tsx: Citation links point at /files?path=<encodeURIComponent(citation)> (line 99). Currently a dead end (no ?path= reader on the /files route). This subtask closes it: FilesAdminView reads ?path= via useSearchParams and highlights/scrolls to the matching catalog row.
dependents.GraphView.tsx: Its own header comment says 'FilesView (6.1.4) does not exist yet, so a future FilesView link can point straight at /graph?path=<path>' -- this subtask's FilesAdminView should link each catalog row to /graph?path=<path> to close that forward-reference too (bonus, not a strict acceptance criterion, but directly implied by the carried-forward context and GraphView's own comment).

no_go_handler_yet.note: Same situation 6.1.3 found for /graph: api/routes/query.go is the only Go handler; api/main.go's newMux only registers a single /query route. This subtask defines the UI-side contract via a disclosed-choice comment, same convention as GraphView.tsx.
